name: @trpc/server
summary: End-to-end type-safe APIs — share TypeScript types between server and client without code generation.

story.problem: You write a REST endpoint and manually duplicate its input/output types in the frontend. They drift apart. A field rename breaks the client silently. You add Zod schemas for validation, then OpenAPI for docs, then a codegen step to sync types. It's a lot of ceremony for what's just a function call.
story.mental_model: tRPC removes the HTTP abstraction between frontend and backend. You define procedures (queries/mutations) on the server, and call them from the client as if they were local async functions. TypeScript infers all types across the boundary — no schemas, no codegen, no REST at all.
story.when_to_use: Full-stack TypeScript monorepos, Next.js apps where frontend and backend share a codebase, projects where you want type safety without REST/GraphQL ceremony.
story.when_not_to_use: Public APIs consumed by third parties (use REST or GraphQL). Polyglot teams where some consumers aren't TypeScript. Microservices with many separate repos.

alternatives.name: GraphQL (Apollo)
alternatives.reason: Language-agnostic, great for complex data graphs, third-party consumers.

alternatives.name: REST + OpenAPI
alternatives.reason: Standard, interoperable, better for public APIs.

alternatives.name: ts-rest
alternatives.reason: REST with tRPC-like type safety — keeps HTTP semantics for when you need them.

nodes.label: @trpc/server
nodes.summary: Server-side package. Define router, procedures, context.

nodes.label: initTRPC.create()
nodes.summary: Initialize tRPC: `const t = initTRPC.context<Context>().create()`. Returns router/procedure builders.

nodes.label: t.router()
nodes.summary: Group procedures: `appRouter = t.router({ getUser: ..., createUser: ... })`. Routers are nestable.

nodes.label: t.procedure (query/mutation)
nodes.summary: query for reads, mutation for writes. `.input(zodSchema).query(({ input, ctx }) => ...)`.

nodes.label: Context
nodes.summary: Per-request data (user session, DB client) injected into every procedure via `createContext`.

nodes.label: Middleware (.use)
nodes.summary: `.use(authMiddleware)` — runs before procedure. Used for auth, logging, rate limiting.

nodes.label: @trpc/client
nodes.summary: Client package. `createTRPCProxyClient` calls server procedures as typed async functions.

nodes.label: @trpc/react-query
nodes.summary: React hooks integration: `trpc.getUser.useQuery({ id: 1 })` wraps React Query automatically.

tasks.title: Define a tRPC router with queries

steps.label: Init
steps.code:
import { initTRPC } from '@trpc/server'
import { z } from 'zod'

const t = initTRPC.create()
export const router = t.router
export const publicProcedure = t.procedure
